codeReview.summary: I reviewed the original failing weather-engine review/diff and the fix commit. The fix flips `calculate_wind_factor()` to the same meteorological source-direction convention used by `src/clients/weather_client.py`, and the updated regression tests now cover both blowing-out/blowing-in sign expectations and parity with the client helper. The original blocking issue is resolved, and I did not find any new blocking problems in the touched code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `feature-worker` still does not reflect the repo's documented Windows-safe startup/typecheck workflow, so the worker again had to deviate to manifest-backed Windows commands even though that exception is now shared knowledge.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\skills\feature-worker\SKILL.md:50` still prescribes `python -m py_compile src/features/*.py` and does not mention the Windows shell workaround; `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\environment.md:57-60` documents the Git Bash / `.factory/init.sh` pitfall; handoff `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-19T19-50-36-774Z__fix-t14-wind-sign-convention__e5895264-4f95-4d87-afbd-964812f2af74.json` records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: false` because the worker used the Windows-specific manifest flow instead.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the prior failing review (`t14-weather-engine`), the original and fix diffs, the fix handoff, and the fix transcript skeleton. Commit `0faf530a91642245acf08081240fb9121c23c3eb` correctly changes the adjustment wind projection to match the weather client's meteorological source-direction semantics and adds regression coverage proving the signs now align, so the original blocking defect is adequately resolved without any new blocking review findings.
